title: 8 Reconciling and Tracing Canadian Dollar Money Orders
body_text:
8	Reconciling and Tracing Canadian Dollar Money Orders
8.1	Overview of Reconciling and Tracing Canadian Dollar Money Orders
Reconcile issued Money Orders to cleared Money Orders on a daily basis.
Since Money Orders are drawn on the credit union and the credit union has online access to the images, it can perform its own traces.
8.2	Reconciling Canadian Dollar Money Orders
Canadian dollar Money Orders are drawn directly on the credit union. The credit union is debited for the Money Order when it is presented for payment or is cashed and settled though the Clearing. Money Orders are processed, settled, and reported with the credit unionâs regular Canadian dollar clearing items.
Ensure that you reconcile cashed Money Orders daily. Credit unions are responsible for monitoring these items for fraudulent activity and returning any such items within the timelines specified by the CPA.
Proceed as follows to reconcile cashed Canadian dollar Money Orders:
Print an image of the cleared Money Order from the Online Return System (ORS).
Note: 	Use the account number MICR encoded on the bottom of the Money Order to search for and view the Money Order on ORS. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures on searching for, viewing, and printing payment items.
Remove copy 3 of the Money Order from the Outstanding Money Orders File and attach the image to it.
File the image and copy 3 of the Money Order in the Completed Money Orders File sequentially by serial number.
8.3	Tracing Canadian Dollar Money Orders
Since Canadian dollar Money Orders are drawn on the credit union, you donât need to issue a trace request to determine if a Money Order is paid. Money Orders are processed with your regular Canadian dollar clearing items. Use ORS to search for and print images of cleared Money Orders.
Note: 	Use the account number MICR encoded on the bottom of the Money Order to search for and view the Money Order on ORS. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures on searching for, viewing, and printing payment items.
END
